// EMAN NASIR MALIK

Personal Profile

Computer Science student who thinks in interfaces — where clean code meets clean design, and both meet an audience that actually notices.
Let's connect → View Resume ↗
EM
Eman Nasir Malik
BS Computer Science · COMSATS University, Wah Campus · 5th Semester

My Story

I live at the intersection of logic and aesthetics — one half of me is a Computer Science student who enjoys the discipline of solving a problem in clean, working code, and the other half is a designer who can't leave that solution alone until it looks and feels right too.

That mix is what pulls me toward UI/UX and graphic design: I like building things people actually want to use, not just things that technically work. Add social media marketing into the picture, and it's really one skill — understanding what draws people in, whether that's a well-structured function or a well-placed pixel.

I believe in practical execution over grades. A transcript can't build, design, or ship — working projects can. That's the standard I hold myself to.

EDUCATION
BS Computer Science
COMSATS University Islamabad, Wah Campus
Wah Cantt, Pakistan · 5th Semester

LOOKING FORWARD TO
Sharpening my skills across development, design, and marketing — and using that mix to land a role where I can build products end to end, not just write the code behind them.

// UI/UX CASE STUDY

Before & After Redesigns

16 website and app redesign concepts across fashion, e-commerce, education, and corporate sectors — each showing a before/after transformation focused on visual hierarchy, cleaner layouts, and stronger brand identity. Click any card to view it full size.

Kids' Gift Sets AppUI/UX
Redesigned a flat, low-contrast shopping screen into a playful, colorful layout with clear category tabs and larger product imagery for easier browsing.
Retro Fashion Shopping AppUI/UX
Reimagined a plain product grid with a nostalgic 50s-70s film-strip visual concept, matching the vintage clothing collection's theme.
Sustainable Fashion Brand WebsiteUI/UX
Restructured dense content into clear sections — Ethical Manufacturing, Organic Cotton, Recycling — with strong icons and an earthy palette.
Sportswear Website RedesignUI/UX
Turned a cluttered, text-heavy layout into a bold, high-contrast hero banner with clear CTAs and organized product categories.
Luxury Menswear WebsiteUI/UX
Refined a cluttered menswear e-commerce layout into a bold, full-bleed editorial design with simplified navigation for a premium feel.
Fashion Retail HomepageUI/UX
Reduced visual clutter, introduced a strong hero image, and organized products into a clean, scannable grid.
Student Portal DashboardUI/UX
Reorganized sidebar navigation and dashboard cards for quicker access to classes, deadlines, notifications, and quick links.
Luxury Fashion Brand HomepageUI/UX
Simplified a busy grid layout into a clean, editorial hero section with elegant typography and better visual breathing room.
University Homepage (Mobile)UI/UX
Replaced a busy grid of small photos with one powerful campus image and clear, action-driven CTAs.
University Footer RedesignUI/UX
Turned an empty, unfinished footer into a structured layout with clear links, contact details, and social icons.
Event & Lecture Listing PageUI/UX
Converted a plain text list into visual cards with images and clear "Learn More" actions, improving scannability.
University Admissions PageUI/UX
Broke down a long wall of text into clear cards — Undergraduate, Graduate, International — with dual CTAs for faster action.
SaaS Dashboard RedesignUI/UX
Rebuilt a dense, text-heavy dashboard into an icon-led card grid with a clean hero section for faster scanning.
University Departments PageUI/UX
Replaced a plain text list of departments with colorful icon cards, each with a short description for quick recognition.
Business Consultancy WebsiteUI/UX
Shifted from a heavy red theme to a calmer, more professional blue palette with organized service cards.
Corporate Website RedesignUI/UX
Simplified a text-dense layout into a professional hero section with improved readability and visual hierarchy.

// PROJECT VAULT

Builds & Projects

Six projects across data structures, OOP, AI security, networking, and databases — with what each one taught me along the way.
UNO — Card Game
C++ / DSA
A console-based UNO game built as a data structures project, using core DS concepts to manage the deck, hands, and turn logic.
LEARNED — how to model real game rules with the right data structure, and how fast a project grows once edge cases and turn logic pile up.
↗ view source
Grading Management System
Java / OOP
A Java application built with object-oriented principles for recording student marks and automatically calculating grades and results.
LEARNED — how OOP concepts like encapsulation and class design make a system easier to extend without breaking what already works.
↗ view source
Choose Your Path
Python
A text-based, branching-choice adventure game in Python, where every decision leads the player down a different story path.
LEARNED — how to structure branching logic cleanly, so the story doesn't collapse into a tangle of nested if-else statements.
↗ view source
LLM Security Gateway
AI / Security
A system built to secure interactions with large language models — guarding against misuse and unsafe inputs while keeping the model reliable.
LEARNED — how to think about a system from an attacker's perspective, and spot where it's exposed before someone else does.
↗ view source
Client-Server Network
Python / Networks
A Computer Networks course project implementing client-server communication in Python, covering how requests and data are exchanged between the two ends.
LEARNED — how data actually travels between two machines, and how many small things can go wrong in that handshake.
↗ view source
Ride Sharing Database
SQL / DBMS
A relational database project modeling a ride-sharing system — designing the schema, relationships, and queries behind riders, drivers, and trips.
LEARNED — how to design a schema that holds up under real relationships and queries, not just one that looks right in a diagram.
↗ view source

// SOCIAL MEDIA MARKETING

Campaigns & Strategy

Three mock campaign case studies built from scratch — each with a clear objective, a content strategy, a weekly calendar, and analytics that show what moved the needle.

01
NovaSkin Cosmetics
Instagram & TikTok Growth Campaign
Instagram TikTok Reels
🎯 Objective

Grow NovaSkin’s Instagram from 4,200 to 10,000 followers in 90 days while driving a 3× increase in website traffic from social channels. Target audience: women aged 18–34 interested in skincare routines and clean beauty.

📋 Strategy
  • Content pillars: Education (how-to skincare), Transformation (before/after), Community (UGC reposts), Product (launches & restocks)
  • Posting cadence: 1 Reel + 1 carousel + 3 Stories per day
  • Hook formula: Open with a pain point → deliver value → soft CTA
  • Hashtag strategy: Mix of niche + broad + branded tags
  • Influencer micro-collab: 5 nano-influencers (5K–20K followers) for authentic UGC seeding
📅 Content Calendar (Week 1)
DayInstagramTikTokStoriesGoal
MonSkincare routine ReelPOV: morning routinePoll: oily vs dry skinAwareness
WedIngredient spotlight carouselDuet with nano-influencerQ&A stickerEducation
FriBefore/after transformationProduct unboxingCountdown: new dropConversion
SunUGC repost + captionComment reply videoBehind the scenesCommunity
📈 Results (90 Days)
+142%
Follower Growth
9.8%
Avg Engagement Rate
3.2×
Website Traffic Lift
41K
Reel Impressions (peak)
02
PulseGym Fitness
Multi-Platform Launch Campaign
Instagram LinkedIn Facebook
🎯 Objective

Generate 500+ pre-registrations for a new gym location opening in 60 days. Secondary goal: build a local community of fitness enthusiasts and position PulseGym as the premium option vs. budget chains.

📋 Strategy
  • Phase 1 (Days 1–20): Teaser campaign — silhouette reveals, countdown posts, “Something big is coming” copy
  • Phase 2 (Days 21–45): Full reveal + early-bird offer (first 100 sign-ups get 20% off)
  • Phase 3 (Days 46–60): Social proof push — testimonials, trainer spotlights, facility tour Reels
  • Facebook Ads: Geo-targeted 5km radius, interest: fitness, gym, health
  • LinkedIn: Corporate wellness angle targeting HR managers for team memberships
📅 Phase Timeline
PhaseInstagramFacebookLinkedInKPI
P1Teaser Reels + countdownAwareness ads (CPM)Thought leadership postReach 20K
P2Grand reveal carouselLead gen ads (CPC)Corporate offer post200 sign-ups
P3Trainer spotlightsRetargeting adsCase study post500 sign-ups
📈 Results (60 Days)
612

100%

EduPath Online Academy
YouTube + LinkedIn Organic Growth
YouTube LinkedIn Twitter/X
🎯 Objective

Drive 5,000 YouTube subscribers and 250+ course enrollments in 120 days through organic content marketing. Target: working professionals aged 25–40 looking to upskill in data science and digital marketing.

📋 Strategy
  • YouTube SEO: Keyword-optimised titles, custom thumbnails (A/B tested), chapters & timestamps
  • Content series: “5-Minute Skills” shorts + 20-min deep-dive tutorials (2×/week)
  • LinkedIn repurposing: Turn each video into a carousel post + text thread the same day
  • Twitter/X threads: Key takeaways from each video as a 7-tweet thread with CTA to full video
  • Email nurture: Weekly digest linking to new content, 28% open rate target
📅 Weekly Content Rhythm
DayYouTubeLinkedInTwitter/XEmail
TueDeep-dive tutorialCarousel repurpose7-tweet thread—
Thu5-min ShortText post + pollTip tweet—
Sat—Community postEngagement replyWeekly digest
